new_classes.label_en: Variety or vintage wine designation quality
new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by a wine that carries a designation indicating grape variety or vintage year, but does not carry a PDO or PGI designation, within the meaning of additional notes 4 and 5 to CN Chapter 22.

new_classes.label_en: Other still wine (variety/vintage designation, no PDO/PGI), actual alcoholic strength ≤ 15% vol, in containers > 2 l and ≤ 10 l (CN 2204 22 98)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Stillwein (Rebsorte/Jahrgang, ohne g.U./g.g.A.), tatsächlicher Alkoholgehalt ≤ 15 % vol, in Behältnissen > 2 l bis einschließlich 10 l (KN 2204 22 98)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes, neither a sparkling wine nor a semi-sparkling wine, bearing a variety or vintage designation but no PDO or PGI, with an actual alcoholic strength of not more than 15% vol, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres and not more than 10 litres. Classified under CN 2204 22 98 with reference to additional notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The principal discriminating criteria are captured: (1) wine of fresh grapes (parent class WineFreshGrapesGrapeMust2204), (2) container size > 2 L and ≤ 10 L (MediumContainer2To10L), (3) variety/vintage designation but neither PDO nor PGI (VarietyOrVintageWineQuality + complements of PDOWineQuality and PGIWineQuality), (4) not sparkling or semi-sparkling (complements of SparklingWineQuality and SemiSparklingWineQuality), and (5) actual alcoholic strength ≤ 15% vol (decimalRange restriction). The legal notes reference additional notes 4 and 5 of Chapter 22 which define total dry extract and its non-volatile constituents (sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matter and salts) as criteria for subheading discrimination within the 2204 22 22–98 range. These compositional/extract thresholds from additional notes 4 and 5 are partially covered by the TotalDryExtractQuality class already in the TBox, but the specific numeric thresholds from additional notes 4(A) (e.g., total dry extract ≥ or < specific g/l values that separate 22042222 from 22042225, 22042231, 22042298 etc.) cannot be fully encoded without the complete text of additional notes 4 and 5. A 20% coverage gap remains for the precise total-dry-extract and residual sugar thresholds that distinguish 22042298 ('other') from its siblings 22042222–22042293 within the same subheading range.
